title: Queue result envelope, schema v2 (additive over v1)
description: Governs results/<id>.json. V1 results (no schema_version key) keep every field verbatim: common fields (job_note, id, host, keepawake_until, steam_mode, finished_at, tier, queued_at, claimed_at, queue_wait_s, run_s), tier1 fields (verdict GREEN/FAIL/REFUSED, status_line, evidence, userdir, isolation, launcher_divergence, steam_mode_before, locked_at_start/end, failed_rules, failure_class), gui fields (verdict COMPLETED/DISPATCH_ERROR/CANCELLED, checks_verdict, failure_class, probe_candidate, status_line, verdict_text(+_file), cost_usd, dispatch_mode, dispatch_events, gui_metrics, checklist(+_ref), preflight_foreground, idle_extensions), plus requeued_as (the ONE sanctioned post-publish mutation), duplicate_of, auto-retry stamps. V2 adds identity/profile/lane/status-taxonomy fields. Additive only; readers select by name; results are never overwritten (dup-naming preserved).

machine.description: Alias of v1 'host' for the generic envelope; both carry the executing hostname. v1 'host' stays authoritative for legacy readers.

effective_profile_hash.description: Recomputed at run time by the profile runtime; MUST equal the job's submit-time hash or the run is a profile-resolution failure.

status.description: The v2 failure taxonomy (design doc 5.4). Distinct from 'verdict' (which keeps its v1 per-tier vocabulary). A passing verdict never hides incomplete cleanup or restoration: if cleanup/restoration failed, status MUST say so even when checks passed.

environment.description: Recorded environment differences (lane equivalence permits differing environments but requires reporting them).

isolation_report.description: What isolation was ACTUALLY enforced (design doc 3.9's isolation block). Requested-but-unenforceable isolation is reported here, never silently dropped. Deliberately NOT named 'isolation': v1 tier1 results already use that key as a STRING (HONORED/IGNORED/AMBIGUOUS) and existing keys are never re-typed — the v1 field stays untouched on Stellaris results.

amendments_applied.description: The structured amendments that entered the effective hash (same shape as job.amendments).
